note: HELD. Never imported by measure.mjs. score.mjs reads this to grade the channels.

predictions.live_engine_fixed_reserve: FALSIFIES the claim: all three streams land at ~the same reserve at the final cursor (a function of step-count, not novelty rate); the reserve does NOT separate stream_t from stream_m and does NOT fall across stream_q's shift.
predictions.calibrated_reserve: CONFIRMS the claim: stream_t reserve >> stream_m reserve at the final cursor (separation); stream_q reserve is high through the burst and FALLS after unit 6 (tracks the recency-weighted rate, where a cumulative counter would stay ~0.5).
predictions.fitness: Forward novelty log-loss -[y log r + (1-y) log(1-r)], y = step-is-novel, r = reserve, aggregated over all cursors of all streams, is LOWER for the calibrated reserve than the fixed reserve. The win must come mostly from stream_t (where the fixed reserve under-reserves a high novelty rate).

thresholds.separation: calibrated stream_t final reserve - calibrated stream_m final reserve > 0.3, while |fixed stream_t - fixed stream_m| < 0.02
thresholds.control_fall: calibrated stream_q reserve at unit 11 < 0.5 * its reserve at unit 5
thresholds.fitness_gain: mean calibrated log-loss < mean fixed log-loss
